title: Gemini and the AI Landscape: Beyond ChatGPT's First-Mover Advantage
description: ChatGPT won the public narrative. But the actual AI competition is far more nuanced—and OpenAI's dominance is fragile. What Gemini, Claude, and others are doing differently.
author: Staff

title: Google Maps: How Location Data Reshapes Urban Planning, Privacy, and Power
description: Google Maps promised directions. It delivered unprecedented surveillance of human movement. What location data reveals—and who has access to it.
author: Staff

title: Instagram and Mental Health: The Hidden Cost of Connection
description: Instagram promised connection. Instead, it delivered comparison, anxiety, and algorithmic manipulation. What research actually shows—and what the platform doesn't want you to know.
author: Staff

title: Cricket's Global Obsession: Sport as Cultural Identity and Economic Power
description: Why 3 billion people follow cricket—not as sport, but as cultural identity. How cricket became the largest sport by audience, revenue, and geopolitical influence.
author: Staff

title: E-Commerce Giants: How Amazon and Flipkart Reshaped Retail and Labor
description: From disruption to dominance—how Amazon and Flipkart conquered retail markets, destroyed traditional merchants, and created a new underclass of gig workers.
author: Staff

title: Gold as Inflation Hedge: When Precious Metals Matter (And When They Don't)
description: Why gold remains valuable in uncertain economies—not as get-rich-quick, but as inflation protection. Historical data, current context, and when to actually buy.
author: Staff

title: Restaurant Culture Post-Pandemic: Community, Economics, and the Future of Dining
description: How restaurants transformed during pandemic lockdowns and what the post-2024 landscape reveals about urban community, labor, and local economics.
author: Staff

title: Why Satta King Traps Millions: The Economics of Desperation
description: Understanding satta king's hold on millions—not as vice, but as rational choice under economic pressure. The systemic failures that make illegal gambling the only escape.
author: Staff

title: Streaming Wars: How Entertainment Distribution Reshapes Culture and Labor
description: From Netflix disruption to streaming saturation—how on-demand video changed entertainment economics, collapsed piracy, and created an unsustainable content arms race.
author: Staff

title: Translation and Globalization: How Breaking Language Barriers Reshapes Power
description: From human translators to AI translation—how breaking language barriers reshapes economies, culture, and global power structures. What's gained, what's lost.
author: Staff

title: The Weather Crisis: Climate Data You Need to Know (And Why You Don't)
description: Climate change isn't a future threat—it's reshaping weather today. The data everyone ignores and why climate literacy matters more than carbon reduction rhetoric.
author: Staff

title: ChatGPT in Perspective
description: Understanding ChatGPT's impact beyond the hype—historical context, labor implications, and systemic changes.
author: Staff

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
description: Explores the two systems of thought that drive the way we think—System 1, which is fast and intuitive, and System 2, which is slow and deliberate. Kahneman reveals the biases and heuristics that often lead us astray.
author: Daniel Kahneman

title: Spanish (Español)
description: Spanish is a Romance language spoken by over 500 million people worldwide, making it the second-most spoken language by number of native speakers. It is the official language of Spain and most countries in Central and South America.
